Field description: If HED tags are used:
The version of the HED schema used to validate HED tags for study.
May include a single schema or a base schema and one or more library schema.

Field description: The license for the dataset.
The use of license name abbreviations is RECOMMENDED for specifying a license
(see [Licenses](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/licenses.md)).
The corresponding full license text MAY be specified in an additional
`LICENSE` file.
Field description: Used to specify provenance of the dataset.

Field description: Used to specify the locations and relevant attributes of all source datasets (BIDS or not).
Valid keys in each object include `"URL"`, `"DOI"` (see
[URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)), and
`"Version"` with
[string](https://www.w3schools.com/js/js_json_datatypes.asp)
values.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: Information describing the receiver coil.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 1250](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1250))
`Receive Coil Name`,
although not all vendors populate that DICOM Tag,
in which case this field can be derived from an appropriate
private DICOM field.

Field description: Information describing the active/selected elements of the receiver coil.
This does not correspond to a tag in the DICOM ontology.
The vendor-defined terminology for active coil elements can go in this field.

Field description: (If used)
A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in
analog the signals from multiple coil elements.
There are typically different default modes when using un-accelerated or
accelerated (for example, `"GRAPPA"`, `"SENSE"`) imaging.

Field description: Almost all fMRI studies using phased-array coils use root-sum-of-squares
(rSOS) combination, but other methods exist.
The image reconstruction is changed by the coil combination method
(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.
Field description: A general description of the pulse sequence used for the scan.

Field description: Variant of the ScanningSequence.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0021](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0021))
`Sequence Variant`.

Field description: Boolean stating if the image saved has been corrected for gradient
nonlinearities by the scanner sequence.

Field description: Type of sequence readout.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0023](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0023))
`MR Acquisition Type`.

Field description: The frequency offset of the magnetization transfer pulse with respect to the
central H1 Larmor frequency in Hertz (Hz).
Field description: The excitation bandwidth of the magnetization transfer pulse in Hertz (Hz).
Field description: The number of magnetization transfer RF pulses applied before the readout.

Field description: Shape of the magnetization transfer RF pulse waveform.
The value `"GAUSSHANN"` refers to a Gaussian pulse with a Hanning window.
The value `"SINCHANN"` refers to a sinc pulse with a Hanning window.
The value `"SINCGAUSS"` refers to a sinc pulse with a Gaussian window.
Field description: Duration of the magnetization transfer RF pulse in seconds.

Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

Field description: The name of the institution in charge of the equipment that produced the
measurements.

Field description: The address of the institution in charge of the equipment that produced the
measurements.

Field description: The department in the institution in charge of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.
